en.value:
Component 1: "Learn, understand, act"


Under Expected Result 1: The concept of WPC is disseminated / started being institutionalized

Activity 1.1:  Institutionalization of Nigarani Samuha/WPC at district level


	Meeting with concerned agencies (at district level) for promotion on the Nigarani Samuha/WPC concept.
	Usage of local media for advocacy of Nigarani Samuha/WPC
	Coordination meeting with District anti-trafficking network for promotion of Nigarani Samuha/WPC
	Interaction with local community leaders, key personnel's in the community for the advocacy of Nigarani Samuha/WPC.
	Development of a toolkit / guideline regarding the WPC concept (and translation of the toolkit)
	Pre test of the toolkit
	Dissemination of the toolkit at district and national level


Activity 1.2:  Institutionalization at national level


	Availibility of the toolkit will help in increasing the State responsibility and accountability showing them that community based non-costly mechanisms can be set up to address TIP and GBV issues.



	Follow up with DWC and ministry to replicate the concept in all district of the country.
	Organisation of training/workshop by PE to disseminate the concept to other NGOs & district authorities.


Under Result 2: 45 Nigarani Samuha and 5 WPCs are created under the Women Cooperative of Makwanpur district and are capacitated to support victims and refer cases

Activity 2.1: Support to WCO to form functional and sustainable Nigarani Samuha/WPC who assist victims and refer cases to existing assistance mechanisms


	Orientation to WCO staff on the toolkit
	Co-facilitation with WCO staff to Women Cooperatives Board Members on Psychosocial counseling,    TIP, GBV, legal processes, Referral mechanisms - 5 days training
	Co-facilitation with WCO staff to Women Cooperatives Board Members on Psychosocial counseling, TIP, GBV, legal processes, Referral mechanisms - 2 days Follow up training
	Monitoring and supervision of assistance mechanism, cases identification, individual counseling, legal actions and referral done by Nigarani samuha/WPC
	Training on Men Engage for Men & GBV for mix groups (men and women) in each VDC
	Parental Education to members of Nigarani Samuha and WCs groups
	Awareness-raising at VDC-level
	Exchange/exposure visit of Nigarani Samuha/WPCs (between new Nigarani Samuha/WPC and the existing Nigarani Samuha/WPCs)
	VDC-level quarterly coordination meetings between existing WPC, sub-group, WCs, and VDC anti trafficking groups


 

 

Activity 2.2:   Formation (setup) of new adolescents groups


	Meetings with existing WC's adolescents groups for activation and mobilization
	Mobilization of 5 Adolescents groups for better linkage with WCs and Nigarani Samuha/WPCs
	Orientation will conduct life skills, SRH, HIV/AIDS, TIP/GBV (1 day per VDC)


Activity 2.3:   Access psychosocial counseling services for victims of GBV/TIP and referral assistance through counseling centre


	Set up Psychosocial counseling Desk under WC office in each VDC
	Delivery counseling (operating)


Under Expected Result 3: Vulnerable families have increased access to financial and in-kind support to ensure sustainability and reduce risks of trafficking

Activity 3.1:  Promotion of inclusive and quality economic services of the WCs


	Training to newly selected WCs on inclusive and efficient saving and credit mechanism
	Facilitate to develop inclusive policy and bylaws (printing, travel, stationery etc)
	Orientation on entrepreneurship to beneficiaries


Follow up & attend WCs' meetings


	Component 2: "Not for sale"


Under Result 1: Girls and women working in the entertainment sector in KTM or at risk of exploitation have an increased access to services and alternatives available to them

Activity 1.1: Outreach Activities 


	Field work (visits of establishments, follow up)
	Mobile counseling camps
	Weekly psychological counseling
	Awareness-raising


Activity 1.2: Coordination and networking 


	Listing potential and relevant companies to collaborate with, coordination and contacts for the vocational trainings and job placement
	Coordination meetings


Activity 1.3: Empowerment Session 


	Groups set-up, knowledge based training/ life skills training
	Follow up and individual support for alternative professional opportunities


 

Under Result 2: Girls and women are reintegrated after having received holistic support within a shelter

Activity 2.1 Institutionalized through the shelter activities 


	Intake activities
	Medical support
	Psychological services
	NFE class with issue based information
	Recreational Activities


 

Activity 2.2: Social and economical rehabilitation activities


	Career plan and developed for each resident/ case management
	Formal education support
	Vocational training
	Job placement and IGA support


 

Activity 2.3: Pre reintegration and Post reintegration act

en.full: II Phase of Promoting Human Rights: combating trafficking, sexual exploitation and violence against women and children

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
